item.title: cmd_review silently ignores unknown options
item.intent: cmd_review must reject unknown flags immediately — silently discarding options lets typos pass undetected and erodes user trust in the CLI
item.description: cmd_review() parses --update-docs and --since but its catch-all branch is '* ) shift ;;' at line 2414, silently discarding any unrecognized flag. Most other commands (cmd_clean, cmd_backlog_add, etc.) reject unknown options with log_error and return 1. A user who mistypes '--update_docs' or '--since-commit' will get no error — the command runs silently in the wrong mode. Fix: replace the silent '* ) shift' with '* ) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1' to match the established pattern.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_review() and locate the option parsing loop (around line 2410-2416)
Replace the catch-all '* ) shift ;;' with '* ) log_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;'
Verify that valid options --update-docs and --since still work normally
Verify that 'aishore review --typo' now prints an error and exits non-zero
acceptanceCriteria: aishore review --unknown-flag exits with a non-zero status and prints an error message
aishore review --update-docs and aishore review --since <commit> still work correctly
Error message matches the format used by other commands: 'Unknown option: <flag>'
